label: meta:EntityTypeShape
description: Every entity type must have at least one human-readable label and is strictly limited to a specific set of allowed attributes. It may include entity type components and indicate what it is based on. While no arbitrary information can be added, an entity type is also permitted to use standard top-level properties and specific connections to describe what it communicates with, the functions it supports, its associated software product, and its typical features.

label: meta:FunctionShape
description: Every function must have at least one label to serve as a human-readable name. Beyond standard basic information, a function is strictly limited to specifying its function components, the data it uses, the entity types it updates, and any measurable entity types it increases or decreases. No other details are permitted.

label: meta:NotEntityTypeShape
description: Functions and roles must not be classified as subcategories of an entity type.

label: meta:NotRoleShape
description: An entity type or a function cannot be categorized as a specific type of role.

label: meta:NotFunctionShape
description: An entity type or a role must never be classified as a subclass of a function.

label: meta:RoleShape
description: Every role must have at least one human-readable name. A role is strictly limited to a specific set of allowed relationships and cannot have any other custom attributes. Specifically, a role can be responsible for another role, a function, or an entity type. It may also approve functions or entity types, be involved in tasks, or include other roles as components.

label: meta:TopShape
description: Every top class must be categorized as exactly one of three things: an entity type, a function, or a role. Any top class can be linked to other concepts through exact, close, related, broader, or narrower matches, and can be marked as a homonym. Beyond these matching links, each category is strictly limited in what characteristics it can have. An entity type may only include a label, entity type components, and information about what it is based on, while specifically ignoring traits like software products, typical features, supported functions, or communication with other components. A function is restricted to having a label, function components, and relationships showing that it uses, updates, increases, or decreases an entity type. Similarly, a role is limited to having a label, role components, and details about its duties, such as being involved in a task, being responsible for another role, function, or entity type, or having the authority to approve functions and entity types.

label: meta:ApplicationComponentShape
description: Every application component must have at least one human-readable label. Additionally, it may specify which other application components it communicates with and outline the specific functions it supports.
